plainLanguageDescription: I know exactly what I need to do but I cannot make myself start, no matter how much I want to or how important it is.
academicLabel: Executive function difficulty: task initiation impairment
manifestations: Opening an assignment and immediately closing it without writing anything
Spending hours preparing to work without beginning the actual task
Only being able to start when a deadline is extremely close
Feeling paralyzed when facing a blank page or empty document

plainLanguageDescription: I genuinely cannot feel time passing. Deadlines sneak up on me even when I know about them weeks in advance.
academicLabel: Executive function difficulty: temporal processing and time perception impairment
manifestations: Losing track of how long something has taken
Being shocked when a deadline arrives even though it was on the calendar
Consistently underestimating how long tasks will take
Missing appointments despite setting reminders

plainLanguageDescription: I forget what I was doing mid-task. I lose instructions the moment I try to act on them. I cannot hold multiple pieces of information in my head at once.
academicLabel: Working memory deficit affecting academic task completion
manifestations: Forgetting verbal instructions before finishing writing them down
Losing track of where I am in a multi-step task
Rereading the same paragraph repeatedly because it does not stay in my mind
Forgetting what I was about to say mid-sentence during presentations

plainLanguageDescription: I can hear words but my brain struggles to process them fast enough, especially when there is background noise or when someone speaks quickly.
academicLabel: Auditory processing difficulty affecting comprehension of verbal instruction
manifestations: Understanding individual words but missing the meaning of full sentences when spoken
Losing the thread of a lecture when there is background noise
Needing instructions repeated multiple times before they make sense
Struggling in discussions where multiple people speak quickly

plainLanguageDescription: Reading takes me much longer than it seems to take other people. By the time I finish a paragraph I have often lost the meaning of the beginning.
academicLabel: Reading fluency and decoding difficulty consistent with dyslexia profile
manifestations: Reading the same sentence multiple times before understanding it
Losing place frequently when reading dense text
Taking significantly longer than peers to complete assigned readings
Words appearing to move or blur on the page when tired

plainLanguageDescription: I understand the material clearly in my head but I struggle to get my thoughts organized and onto paper in a way that matches what I actually know.
academicLabel: Written expression difficulty: organization and transcription impairment
manifestations: Knowing the answer but being unable to structure it in writing
Written work that does not reflect actual understanding of the subject
Spending disproportionate time on written assignments compared to peers
Ideas feeling clear until the moment I try to write them down

plainLanguageDescription: Certain environments make it almost impossible to concentrate. Noise, lighting, or too much activity around me can completely shut down my ability to think.
academicLabel: Sensory processing sensitivity affecting ability to function in standard academic environments
manifestations: Fluorescent lighting causing headaches or difficulty focusing
Background conversations being impossible to filter out
Physical discomfort from classroom sensory environment affecting concentration
Needing to leave environments that become overwhelming without warning

plainLanguageDescription: I find it difficult to navigate unwritten social rules in academic settings, like knowing when to speak in class, how to approach professors, or what group work expectations really mean.
academicLabel: Social communication difficulty affecting participation in standard academic interactions
manifestations: Not knowing when it is appropriate to ask a question in class
Misunderstanding assignment instructions that rely on implied expectations
Finding group work difficult due to unclear roles and unspoken norms
Avoiding office hours due to uncertainty about how those interactions work

plainLanguageDescription: Switching between tasks, classes, or environments is genuinely hard for me. Unexpected changes to routines can derail my entire day.
academicLabel: Cognitive flexibility difficulty: transition and routine change impairment
manifestations: Needing extra time to mentally shift between subjects or tasks
Schedule changes causing significant distress and loss of productivity
Difficulty leaving one task to begin another even when time requires it
Unexpected changes to class format or location causing shutdown

plainLanguageDescription: Breaking large projects into steps and managing multiple deadlines at once is extremely difficult. Everything feels equally urgent or I cannot figure out where to begin.
academicLabel: Executive function difficulty: planning, organization, and prioritization impairment
manifestations: Looking at a large assignment and not knowing which part to do first
Losing track of multiple deadlines across different courses
Submitting incomplete work because the planning stage never finished
Feeling overwhelmed by projects that peers seem to manage easily

plainLanguageDescription: I struggle to keep my attention on something for a sustained period, especially when the task is not immediately engaging, even when I genuinely want to focus.
academicLabel: Sustained attention difficulty affecting completion of extended academic tasks
manifestations: Mind wandering during lectures despite genuine interest in the subject
Losing focus during exams and having to reread questions multiple times
Inconsistent performance that does not reflect actual knowledge
Hyperfocusing on interesting tasks while being unable to engage with others

plainLanguageDescription: Stressful academic situations can cause emotional responses that feel out of proportion and make it hard to continue working or think clearly.
academicLabel: Emotional regulation difficulty affecting academic functioning under stress
manifestations: Exam anxiety that causes blanking despite knowing the material
Criticism or feedback triggering shutdown rather than adjustment
Difficulty recovering and returning to work after an upsetting event
Avoidance of assessments due to fear of the emotional response they cause

plainLanguageDescription: I understand the material but I need more time than most people to process information and produce responses. Speed-based assessments do not reflect what I actually know.
academicLabel: Processing speed deficit affecting timed academic performance
manifestations: Running out of time on exams despite knowing the answers
Taking longer than peers to respond in discussions
Needing to hear or read something multiple times before it fully registers
Written output that is slower than thinking speed

plainLanguageDescription: I find it hard to express what I know verbally, especially under pressure. I understand more than I can say out loud in the moment.
academicLabel: Verbal expression difficulty affecting oral participation and assessment performance
manifestations: Knowing an answer but being unable to retrieve the words quickly enough
Performing significantly better on written than oral assessments
Anxiety about being called on in class affecting participation
Verbal responses not accurately representing actual knowledge

plainLanguageDescription: I know I struggle but I have never been formally diagnosed, or my diagnosis is old, or I cannot afford to get updated documentation. I should not have to prove myself to get support.
academicLabel: Undocumented or under-documented neurodivergence with clear functional impact
manifestations: Experiencing clear functional barriers without formal diagnosis
Having a diagnosis but lacking recent documentation required by the institution
Being unable to afford a formal evaluation
Coming from a background where neurodivergence was not recognized or assessed
